Truth Unlocked, Volume 1: Christian Belief

Eight studies to help you understand the essential truths revealed by God in the Bible. We cannot live godly lives unless we understand Biblical truth and apply it as the foundation for our lives. Truth Unlocked is also a helpful refresher for those who have been Christians for some time and wish to review the basics of their faith. You may be surprised by what you learn!

Conversion: New not (just) Nice

A nine-session Bible study exploring what genuine conversion means – and why it matters.
Character, Conviction & Competence for Christian Ministry

Truth Unlocked, Volume 2: Christian Living

Eight studies to help you understand the essential truths revealed by God in the Bible. We cannot live godly lives unless we understand Biblical truth and apply it as the foundation for our lives. Truth Unlocked is also a helpful refresher for those who have been Christians for some time and wish to review the basics of their faith. You may be surprised by what you learn!

Grow in Christ (Discipleship course)

Are you growing in Christ? God wants all his children to grow in Jesus. If you are not growing, this studies will help you work out what is wrong and how to put it right. If you are growing, they will help you grow more. Working through Scripture, each session combines close reading with personal reflection. (We have split this course into several volumes. Do volume 1 first – but if you've already done that, carry on with the next volume.)

A ten-session Bible study exploring the heart behind addiction — and how the Gospel offers more than any false promise can deliver. Working through Scripture, each session combines close reading with personal reflection.

studyTitles.description: Leadership, labor, and rebuilding for God's glory.

Working for the Good: Faith, Love, and Worship in our Everyday Labour

An eight-session Bible study exploring what it means to work faithfully as a Christian. Drawing on passages from Genesis to Revelation, each session tackles a different dimension of work: its God-given goodness, its distortion by sin, and its redemption in Christ. Whether you feel your job is meaningless, disconnected from your faith, or simply exhausting, this study invites you to see your daily labour as an act of love, a form of worship, and part of God's larger purposes for the world.
